context_values.response.debate:
The final plan has been written to `.workflow/plan_final.md` (936 lines). Here's a summary of the synthesis decisions made:

## Synthesis Summary

### Structure: 8-phase work queue as requested
1. **Scaffold + Scene** — Three.js boot, game loop, input, placeholder kart
2. **Track Geometry + Rendering** — Spline system, road mesh, walls, all 4 tracks, surface detection
3. **Kart Physics + Driving Model** — Character stats, kart-kart collision, slipstream, 8 character models
4. **Drift/Boost System** — 3-tier drift, boost mechanics, sparks, camera effects
5. **Items + Pickups** — 6 items, item boxes, roulette, position-weighted distribution
6. **AI Opponents** — Spline following, PD steering, drift AI, item AI, 3 difficulty presets
7. **HUD + Menus + Audio** — Full UI flow, HUD, minimap, pause, results, procedural Web Audio
8. **Polish + Textures** — Particles, camera polish, hazards, mirror mode, performance, imagegen

### What was taken from each plan

| Decision | Source | Reason |
|----------|--------|--------|
| Separate `textures.js` module | GPT plan | Cleaner separation of concerns |
| Separate `drift.js` module | Both (agreed) | Complex enough to warrant its own file |
| Quantitative difficulty presets with numbers | GPT plan | More implementable than qualitative descriptions |
| Deliverable checklists per phase | GPT plan | Provides clear acceptance criteria |
| Detailed per-subsection breakdowns | Opus plan | More thorough module-level detail |
| Dependency graph | Opus plan | Clearer visualization of build order |
| File size estimates | Opus plan | More realistic (7.5k vs 4.4k) |
| Cross-cutting concerns section | GPT plan | Important edge cases addressed explicitly |
| Gotchas section | Opus plan | Practical implementation warnings |
| All spec-review fixes incorporated | Both (verified) | 21 fixes from review all addressed |

context_values.graph.goal: Build Fabro Racer — a 3D voxel kart racer using Three.js with 4 tracks, 8 characters, items, drifting, and CPU opponents. Output is static HTML + JS files.
context_values.response.expand_spec:
The spec is written to `.workflow/spec.md`. Here's a summary of what it covers:

**Complete game design spec for Fabro Racer** — 22 sections, ~1,200 lines:

| Section | Key Decisions |
|---------|--------------|
| **Driving Model** | Arcade physics, 28 u/s base speed, 3-tier drift-boost (0.7s/1.1s/1.5s), forgiving wall collisions (glancing=15% scrub, head-on=40%) |
| **4 Tracks** | Sunset Circuit (beginner oval, 32s lap), Fungal Canyon (figure-8 with bridge, 45s), Neon Grid (technical chicanes+jump, 52s), Frostbite Pass (mountain elevation loop, 58s) — each with hazards, shortcuts, AI splines, color palettes |
| **8 Characters** | Blip (handler), Grumble (tank), Zephyr (speedster), Cinder (accel), Tundra (heavy defender), Pixel (balanced), Mossworth (steady), Stardust (wildcard) — 14 total stat points each, unique AI personalities |
| **6 Items** | Spark Orb, Banana Peel, Turbo Mushroom, Homing Pigeon, Oil Slick, Speed Leech — position-weighted distribution, max 1.2s disruption, defensive items holdable as rear shields |
| **AI System** | Spline-following with PD controller, 3 difficulty presets (Chill/Standard/Mean), drift zone awareness, item logic, deliberate mistakes for organic feel |
| **Technical Architecture** | Pure static files, ES modules with import map for Three.js CDN, procedural textures via Canvas, Web Audio API for all sound, fixed-timestep physics at 60Hz, HTML/CSS HUD overlay |
| **Full UI Flow** | Title → Track Select → Character Select → Options → Countdown → Racing → Results, with pause menu, minimap, stat bars, item roulette |
| **9-Phase Build Plan** | Ordered from core driving loop → tracks → drift → AI → items → characters → UI → audio → polish |

| 3. Track loads with geometry | ✅ PASS | Sunset Circuit: 603m length, 303 frames, 8 checkpoints, 18m road width |

**Additional findings:**
- **0 console errors** — clean initialization
- **20 draw calls** — well under the <100 budget
- All 8 character voxel models built and placed on starting grid
- All 4 track definitions present (sunset, fungal, neon, frostbite)
- Drift system logic verified correct (3-tier with proper thresholds)
- Physics values match spec (acceleration, max speed, turn rates, off-road multiplier)
- Fixed 60Hz timestep with interpolated rendering working correctly

context_values.response.review_spec:
All 21 improvements from the review have been applied. Here's a summary of what was done:

## Summary

### Review Written: `.workflow/spec-review.md`
A detailed 21-item review covering 4 categories:
1. **Internal Consistency Issues** (6 items) — stat budgets, duplicate difficulty stars, ambiguous off-road rules, undefined boost comparison, formula overflow, slipstream fairness
2. **Missing Edge Cases** (11 items) — ties, respawns, invincibility, homing pigeon from 1st, roulette overlap, item-item interactions, hazard stacking, wrong-way detection, CPU finish timing, countdown pause, item box activation
3. **Testability Gaps** (3 items) — pack racing enforcement, qualitative AI personalities, missing weight formula
4. **Minor Omissions** (4 items) — loading screen, grid navigation, window resize

### Applied to `spec.md` — All 21 fixes:

**High severity:**
- Fixed 4 characters (Grumble, Zephyr, Mossworth, Stardust) to sum to 14 stat points
- Clarified off-road penalty: only Turbo Mushroom reduces it
- Added **Section 3.9 Respawn** with full mechanics (fade out/in, checkpoint teleport, 2.0s cost)
- Added **Section 3.8 Post-Hit Invincibility** (2.0s, alpha blink)
- Fixed race position formula to use normalized [0,1) fractional distance

**Medium severity:**
- Defined boost "stronger" comparison: new initial power vs. current remaining power
- Added **Section 3.7 Slipstream** as universal mechanic (moved from AI-only)
- Added Homing Pigeon behavior from 1st place (fires as straight-line projectile)
- Added roulette-blocks-pickup rule
- Added item-item non-interaction rule
- Added hazard effect stacking/priority rules
- Added wrong-way detection (5s warning, 15s respawn prompt)
- Added quantitative AI personality parameters for all 8 characters
- Added weight bump formula: `baseBumpForce * (otherWeight / selfWeight)`

**Low severity:**
- Fixed difficulty stars to 4-star scale (★☆☆☆ through ★★★★)
- Clarified finish timeout: only for CPU after player finishes; no time limit if player is last
- Disabled pause during countdown; item boxes inactive until GO!
- Added loading screen visuals specification
- Clarified grid navigation for UI menus
- Added window resize handling requirement
- Added catch-up assist mechanic (Chill/Standard only, +1 u/s per position below median)
context_values.response.implement_core:
Here's a summary of what was implemented across Phases 1-4:

## Implementation Complete — Phases 1-4 (Core Engine)

### Files Created (22 files, ~4100 lines of JS)

**Phase 1: Project Scaffold**
| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `output/index.html` | Entry point with Three.js import map (unpkg CDN r170), canvas, HUD layers |
| `output/css/style.css` | Full-viewport canvas, HUD styling, menu classes, speed bar |
| `output/js/main.js` | Boot, WebGL renderer, scene/camera, 60fps fixed-timestep game loop |
| `output/js/state.js` | StateManager + RacingState with game loop integration |
| `output/js/input.js` | Keyboard input manager with polling + edge detection |
| `output/js/utils.js` | Math helpers (lerp, clamp, smoothDamp, wrapAngle) |

**Phase 2: Track System**
| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `output/js/spline.js` | CatmullRomCurve3 utilities, projection, frame sampling |
| `output/js/voxel.js` | Voxel model builder with mergeGeometries, 7 prop types |
| `output/js/textures.js` | Procedural textures: road, off-road, boost pad, checker, ice |
| `output/js/track.js` | Track builder: road mesh, walls, ground, scenery, surface detection |
| `output/js/tracks/sunsetCircuit.js` | 25-point coastal circuit, ~520m |
| `output/js/tracks/fungalCanyon.js` | 35-point cave figure-8, ~680m |
| `output/js/tracks/neonGrid.js` | 40-point neon cityscape, ~740m |
| `output/js/tracks/frostbitePass.js` | 45-point mountain pass, ~850m |

**Phase 3: Kart & Physics**
| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `output/js/kart.js` | Kart entity with state, placeholder model, interpolation |
| `output/js/physics.js` | Arcade physics, wall collision, kart-kart collision, checkpoints |
| `output/js/characters.js` | 8 characters with stats, colors, AI params, voxel models |

**Phase 4: Drift & Boost**
| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `output/js/drift.js` | 3-tier drift system with boost on release |
| `output/js/camera.js` | Spring-damper chase camera with drift swing, boost FOV |

**Textures (imagegen)**
- `output/textures/road.png`, `grass.png`, `dirt.png`

### Verified ✅
- Three.js initializes from CDN, scene renders at 60fps
- Kart visible on track, accelerates to max speed (28 u/s)
- Steering, braking, coasting all work correctly
- Chase camera follows smoothly with spring-damper physics
- Drift: initiates on steer+drift, charges Tier 1→2→3, boost on release (6/8/10 u/s)
- Wall collision detection with forgiving glancing/head-on response
- Kart-kart collision with weight-based separation
- HUD shows position, lap counter, race timer
- No JavaScript errors
